---
title: "Mathematical Finance of Alternative Assets"
subtitle: "Illiquidity, Incomplete Markets, and Stochastic Valuation · Graduate Finance Course"
---

## Course Preview

<!-- Replace VIDEO_ID with your YouTube ID when the introduction videos are ready -->
{{< video https://www.youtube.com/embed/VIDEO_ID_PREVIEW >}}

## Why This Course Exists

Alternative assets now hold a large share of institutional capital, yet the
mathematics used to value them is largely borrowed from markets where prices
are continuous, observation is continuous, and every risk is spanned. None of
those assumptions hold for a buyout fund, a bilateral loan, a toll road, or a
venture portfolio. This course develops the replacement toolkit: stochastic
cash-flow claims, valuation under incompleteness, filtering of smoothed
observations, nonlinear pricing operators, optimal stopping and switching,
and robust portfolio construction — the seventeen chapters of *Mathematical
Finance of Alternative Assets*.

## What You Will Build

- The habit of reporting **valuation distributions**, not point estimates,
  with the operator stated and every premium located at exactly one address
- A working command of the book's valuation operators — from the
  liquidity-adjusted stochastic DCF of Chapter 4 to the entropic robust
  operators of Chapter 17
- Hands-on fluency with the **companion laboratory**: every chapter has a
  webapp module, a Python notebook, and an Excel workbook, all producing
  identical numbers on identical seeds
- Verified computational work: every laboratory module carries the book's
  validation checks, and passing them is part of every assignment

## The Three Companions

| Companion | What it is | Where |
|---|---|---|
| **Laboratory webapp** | 17 chapter dashboards implementing every Webapp Laboratory section of the book | [Open the Laboratory](https://YOUR-LOVABLE-APP-URL) |
| **Python notebooks** | One notebook per chapter driving the same engine | [Labs & Downloads](labs.qmd) |
| **Excel workbooks** | One workbook per chapter, one tab per computational exercise, live formulas + engine values | [Labs & Downloads](labs.qmd) |

All three consume the same Python engine with the book's seeds
(`2026NNNN`), so the numbers agree by construction.
